title: Scrambled Eggs Timing Calculator
explanation: Adjust egg count, preheat time and butter amount to compute the ideal cooking duration for silky scrambled eggs.

cells:
def cooking_time(egg_count, preheat_minutes=0, butter_g=5):
        seconds_per_egg = 30
        base_seconds = egg_count * seconds_per_egg
        total_seconds = base_seconds + butter_g
        total_seconds += preheat_minutes * 60
        return total_seconds

# Use injected slider variables
 time_sec = cooking_time(egg_count, preheat_minutes, butter_g)
 print(f"Estimated cooking time: {time_sec:.1f} seconds")

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

def plot_time_vs_eggs():
    egg_counts = list(range(1,7))
    times = [cooking_time(n) / 60 for n in egg_counts]  # minutes
    plt.plot(egg_counts, times, marker='o')
    plt.xlabel('Number of Eggs')
    plt.ylabel('Cooking Time (minutes)')
    plt.title('Scrambled Egg Cooking Time')
    plt.grid(True)
    plt.show()

plot_time_vs_eggs()

title: Scrambled Egg Doneness
explanation: Adjusting cooking time lets you see how the eggs transition from creamy to overcooked.
